etymology.description: 囗("fence") + 袁("phonetic cue") -> enclosure for growing plants
etymology.interpretation: The character 園(園) (yuán, "garden") conveys the idea of an enclosed area, represented by 囗(囗) (wéi, "fence"), with 袁(袁) (yuán, "phonetic cue") providing the pronunciation. This combines the concepts of an enclosed space and phonetic similarity to suggest a 'garden'.
etymology.simplification: 園(園) was simplified to 园(园) by replacing 袁(袁) with a simpler phonetic element: the upper component from 袁 was removed, leaving only the important semantic enclosure 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") and a component of 袁 for phonetical cue.

examples.chinese: 她在花园里种了很多玫瑰(她在花園裏種了很多玫瑰)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài huāyuán lǐ zhòng le hěn duō méiguī.
examples.english: She planted many roses in the garden.

examples.chinese: 这个公园非常适合散步(這個公園非常適合散步)。
examples.english: This park is very suitable for walking.

examples.chinese: 我们计划在后院建一个小花园(我們計劃在後院建一個小花園)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen jìhuà zài hòuyuàn jiàn yī gè xiǎo huāyuán.
examples.english: We plan to build a small garden in the backyard.
